title: Architecture and Repository Organization

layers.name: ingest
layers.purpose: Load pinned upstream Signal K schemas and normalize raw metadata
responsibilities: fetch tree and schema documents from pinned upstream ref
capture provenance metadata (sha, source URLs, generatedAt)

layers.name: generation
layers.purpose: Convert Draft-04 JSON Schema to deterministic TypeBox source
responsibilities: map nullable type arrays to Type.Union([X, Type.Null()])
emit Type.Ref() relationships using a central registry
emit deterministic source order and manifests

layers.name: runtime
layers.purpose: Bind generated schema registry to validators and parser without API breakage
responsibilities: preserve parser output unions and status enums
enforce lenient unknown-field handling by default

layers.name: docs
layers.purpose: Generate technical and end-user schema docs from manifests
responsibilities: derive schema index and coverage tables

layers.name: coverage
layers.purpose: Track implementation and parity progress per domain batch
responsibilities: maintain per-batch completion and test parity status

mustKeepStable: createParserRuntime() public factory name and behavior
ParsedValue union branch names and status strings
allowedInternalChanges: schema registry source can move behind compatibility exports
validator compilation may split by domain while preserving createDeltaValidators()
path-to-type indexing may use generated lookup maps
compatibilityRules: do not remove existing exports in a minor release
additive schema support only in non-breaking releases
breaking parser contract changes require major bump

questions.question: Should every generated schema have a stable $id for deterministic Type.Ref resolution?
Under lenient unknown-field policy, should unknown fields be preserved in runtime output?
Should src/lib/schemas.ts remain the public compatibility entry while generated internals stay behind re-exports?
Should CI fail if regeneration produces non-deterministic file diffs?
How should external refs such as geojson.org be handled during generation?

gateBDecisionBlock.oneLineApprovalTemplate: Gate B - Approve with defaults - Apply B1..B5 recommended defaults and proceed to Phase 2
gateBDecisionBlock.decision: Gate B - Approve with defaults - Apply B1..B5 recommended defaults and proceed to Phase 2

gateBReadyChecklist: parser/runtime public API stability contract defined
decisions from Gate A reflected in architecture
